tools.name: search_sip_docs
tools.description:
[cost: rag (one embed + one vector search) | read-only, network: outbound to embed model only]

Vector search over Sipflow's curated VoIP knowledge base: vendor docs (Asterisk, FreeSWITCH, Kamailio, OpenSIPS, Twilio, Cisco, etc.), SIP/SDP/WebRTC RFCs, STIR/SHAKEN material (RFC 8224/8225/8226/8588/90

tools.name: lookup_response_code
tools.description:
[cost: free (pure CPU, no network) | read-only]

Instant static lookup of a SIP response code (100-699). Returns name, RFC anchor, category, description, common operator-flavored causes, and known vendor-specific reason-phrase variants (e.g. OpenSIPS emits 484 'Invalid FROM' on From-header parse fai

tools.name: lookup_sip_header
tools.description:
[cost: free (pure CPU, no network) | read-only]

Instant lookup of a SIP header by canonical or compact form (e.g. "Via" / "v", "Diversion", "P-Asserted-Identity", "Identity", "Session-Expires"). Returns canonical form, compact alias, RFC anchor, where it appears (request / response / both), cardina

tools.name: detect_sip_stack
tools.description:
[cost: free (pure CPU, no network) | read-only]

Identify the SIP product behind a piece of input. Works on both:
  - a SIP trace (User-Agent / Server headers from PCAP/sngrep/syslog), and
  - a vendor config blob (kamailio.cfg, sip.conf, pjsip.conf, FreeSWITCH XML, opensips.cfg) detected via struct

tools.name: detect_sip_vendor_from_config
tools.description:
[cost: free (pure CPU, no network) | read-only]

Heuristic-only sibling of `detect_sip_stack`, scoped to vendor configs. Returns the matched vendor slug, a confidence level, and the structural signals that fired (loadmodule syntax, route blocks, profile elements, etc.).

Use this when the user asks

tools.name: minimize_sip_trace
tools.description:
[cost: free (pure CPU, no network) | read-only, no persistence]

Reduce a raw SIP trace to a compact form suitable for sending to an LLM. Preserves SDP bodies and routing/auth/dialog headers; prunes well-known noise (User-Agent, Server, Allow, Accept-*, Date, P-* informational, etc.).

Expected inpu

tools.name: render_sip_ladder
tools.description:
[cost: free (pure CPU, no network) | read-only]

Parse a raw SIP trace (PCAP-decoded text, sngrep export, syslog, or pasted INVITE/200 dialog) and emit a Mermaid `sequenceDiagram` block visualizing the call flow. Most chat hosts (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, GitHub) render Mermaid inline.

Lane keying:

tools.name: sip_ladder_example
tools.description:
[cost: free (pure CPU, no network) | read-only]

Return a hand-curated SIP scenario as a Mermaid `sequenceDiagram` plus a bullet list of step-by-step explanations with RFC references. Use this when the user asks 'show me what X looks like' and you don't have a real trace handy.

Available scenarios:

tools.name: lint_sip_request
tools.description:
[cost: free (pure CPU, no network) | read-only, no persistence]

Run RFC 3261 / RFC 3325 / RFC 8224 / RFC 8225 / CTIA BCID compliance checks on a single raw SIP request (typically an INVITE) and return a list of findings.

Catches the failure modes that silently break carrier interop:
  - Two `From:

tools.name: parse_sip_message
tools.description:
[cost: free (pure CPU, no network) | read-only, no persistence]

Parse a single raw SIP message (request OR response) and return a structured view: start line (method/status), every header in order with line numbers, body, duplicate-header counts, and a list of structural flags the parser noticed (`

tools.name: diff_sip_messages
tools.description:
[cost: free (pure CPU, no network) | read-only, no persistence]

Take two SIP messages (typically the same request observed at two adjacent hops - e.g. the INVITE leaving FreeSWITCH and the INVITE arriving at Kamailio) and surface a structured per-header diff: `added`, `removed`, `mutated` (with old

tools.name: review_sip_config
tools.description:
[cost: free (pure CPU, no network) | read-only]

Use this when the user asks 'review my config' or attaches a kamailio.cfg, sip.conf, pjsip.conf, FreeSWITCH XML profile, opensips.cfg, `res_fax.conf` / `udptl.conf` / `spandsp.conf` (fax-relay tuning), or a SIP-shaped source file from a repo. This too

tools.name: webrtc_sip_checklist
tools.description:
[cost: free (pure CPU, no network) | read-only]

Return a curated checklist of WebRTC ↔ SIP requirements (WSS transport, ICE gathering, DTLS-SRTP fingerprint, rtcp-mux + BUNDLE, media relay / rtpengine, STUN/TURN, secure-context Origin allowlist, Opus codec, session-timer behavior across the bridge,

tools.name: parse_sdp
tools.description:
[cost: free (pure CPU, no network) | read-only]

Parse a Session Description Protocol body and return a structured view: origin, session, timing, per-media codecs (rtpmap + fmtp), direction, DTLS setup + fingerprint, ICE credentials + candidates, rtcp-mux, BUNDLE groups, fax-relay (`m=image udptl t3

tools.name: compare_sdp_offer_answer
tools.description:
[cost: free (pure CPU, no network) | read-only]

Diff a SIP/SDP offer and answer and surface the issues that actually break calls in practice: codec intersection per m-line, direction compatibility (sendrecv ↔ recvonly), DTLS setup-role conflicts (active+active / passive+passive), rtcp-mux / BUNDLE

tools.name: validate_stir_shaken_identity
tools.description:
[cost: external_io (HTTPS fetch of the x5u cert) | read-only]

Verify a SIP `Identity:` JWS (RFC 8224 / SHAKEN). Fetches the x5u certificate, parses it, verifies the ES256 signature against the cert's public key, and optionally validates the RCD icon hash (RFC 9795). The icon-hash check accepts both

tools.name: stir_attestation_explainer
tools.description:
[cost: free (pure CPU, no network) | read-only]

Static explainer for STIR/SHAKEN: maps attestation levels (A / B / C per RFC 8588) to plain-English requirements + common scenarios, and SIP codes commonly emitted by signing/verification (428 / 436 / 437 / 438 / 608) to their RFC anchors and operator

tools.name: validate_e164_number
tools.description:
[cost: free (pure CPU, no network) | read-only]

Parse a phone number, normalize to E.164, and classify it. International coverage is via libphonenumber-js (every country, line type when known). NANP numbers (CC=1) are additionally split into NPA (area code) / NXX (central office) / station, and tag

tools.name: troubleshoot_response_code
tools.description:
[cost: rag (one embed + one vector search) | read-only, network: outbound to embed model only | rate-limited per IP]

Like `lookup_response_code` but augmented: returns the static RFC entry PLUS the top vendor-specific RAG hits for the exact code (and any free-text context the user pasted). When the

tools.name: dns_diagnose_sip_target
tools.description:
[cost: external_io (DNS via Cloudflare + Google; TLS handshake + a SIP OPTIONS keepalive to public targets when applicable) | read-only | rate-limited per IP: 10/min, 200/day]

Walk DNS the same way a SIP UA does (RFC 3263 §4.1): NAPTR → SRV → A/AAAA. Given a SIP URI ("sip:example.com"), bare hostna

tools.name: fetch_sipflow_share
tools.description:
[cost: external_io (Mongo + S3 fetch on the Sipflow backend) | read-only, no persistence | rate limit: shared with the public share endpoint]

Given a Sipflow share URL (https://sipflow.dev/share/<token>, or any sipflow.dev subdomain that serves /share/<token>), load the shared SIP trace AND any pri

tools.name: submit_sipflow_feedback
tools.description:
[cost: write (single MongoDB row) | rate-limited per IP: 3/min, 20/day]

Send the Sipflow team feedback when something doesn't work, a vendor or RFC isn't covered, or a tool produced a wrong/incomplete answer. Categories:
  - docs_gap: search_sip_docs returned nothing useful, vendor missing, coverag

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "lookup_response_code" appears informational but accepts parameters (code) that enable code/file/secret access.

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "lint_sip_request" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: [cost: free (pure CPU, no network) | read-only, no persistence] Run RFC 3261 / RFC 3325 / RFC 8224 / RFC 8225 / CTIA BCID compliance checks on a single raw SIP …

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "parse_sdp" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: [cost: free (pure CPU, no network) | read-only] Parse a Session Description Protocol body and return a structured view: origin, session, timing, per-media codec…

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "stir_attestation_explainer" appears informational but accepts parameters (code) that enable code/file/secret access.

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "troubleshoot_response_code" appears informational but accepts parameters (code, context) that enable code/file/secret access.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "dns_diagnose_sip_target" stacks urgency/override words (never, warning). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: [cost: external_io (DNS via Cloudflare + Google; TLS handshake + a SIP OPTIONS keepalive to public targets when applicable) | read-only | rate-limited per IP: 1…

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "submit_sipflow_feedback" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: [cost: write (single MongoDB row) | rate-limited per IP: 3/min, 20/day] Send the Sipflow team feedback when something doesn't work, a vendor or RFC isn't covere…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "submit_sipflow_feedback" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: [cost: write (single MongoDB row) | rate-limited per IP: 3/min, 20/day] Send the Sipflow team feedback when something doesn't work, a vendor or RFC isn't covere…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: search_sip_docs, minimize_sip_trace, render_sip_ladder, lint_sip_request, parse_sip_message, diff_sip_messages, review_sip_config, compare_sdp_offer_answer, fetch_sipflow_share · Private data: detect_sip_vendor_from_config, render_sip_ladder, sip_ladder_example, review_sip_config, parse_sdp, dns_diagnose_sip_target, fetch_sipflow_share, submit_sipflow_feedback · Public sink: parse_sip_message, fetch_sipflow_share, submit_sipflow_feedback · Destructive: minimize_sip_trace, lint_sip_request, review_sip_config, validate_e164_number, fetch_sipflow_share.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: search_sip_docs, minimize_sip_trace, render_sip_ladder, lint_sip_request, parse_sip_message, diff_sip_messages, review_sip_config, compare_sdp_offer_answer, fetch_sipflow_share · Private data: detect_sip_vendor_from_config, render_sip_ladder, sip_ladder_example, review_sip_config, parse_sdp, dns_diagnose_sip_target, fetch_sipflow_share, submit_sipflow_feedback · Public sink: parse_sip_message, fetch_sipflow_share, submit_sipflow_feedback · Destructive: minimize_sip_trace, lint_sip_request, review_sip_config, validate_e164_number, fetch_sipflow_share

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
